ratification_chronology: Pudding #54 drafted Bishop B061 (April 2, 2026). Knight Session 81 (Treasure Map Progression), K118 (Flow), K164 (Flip Cards), K311 (V2 Treasure Map Builder). Trail-head seeding migration 20260502000001 (BP015).
definition: Treasure Maps are craft-specific, living, self-updating guides that replace the traditional business plan. A Treasure Map is short (fits on one page), specific (exact materials, prices, links for each craft), and progressive (5 steps: Materials -> First Piece -> Set Up Project -> Get First Backers -> Scale). Unlike a business plan written for investors, a Treasure Map is written for the member doing the work. It updates in real-time: when Step 1 completes, the Map highlights Step 2; when the first backer arrives, real earnings data replaces projections. Progress persists across sessions. The Adaptive Experience-Gated Onboarding Router (Innovation #1951) routes members to the correct starting step. Each Map contains a craft-specific Economics Panel showing income trajectories at 50 / 500 / 5,000 backers. Treasure Maps are Cold Start tools: a member who completes through Step 4 has materials, a product, a project page, a Cue Card, and five backers -- a functioning micro-business in weeks.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: density holds at 57 hits. Trail Head architecture (BP015) adds Deck Card / QR integration path canonized post-Reckoning. No coverage degradation at 8cP tier.
founder_voice_quotes: "Help each other help ourselves" -- Golden Key of the cooperative; Treasure Maps operationalize this for the creator journey

ratification_chronology: Article 'Why Code-Breakers Matter' (ARTICLE_WHY_CODE_BREAKERS_MATTER_HUMANIZED.md) ratified. Pudding #79 'The Golden Key' (Bishop B062, April 2, 2026). Battery Dispatch: 11 of 15 posts contain hidden keywords.
definition: Code Breakers is the Liana Banyan engagement mechanic that rewards attention rather than extracting it. Hidden keywords called Golden Keys are embedded across all documents. Three difficulty tiers: Fledgling (word appears naturally, 1 feather), Flight (first letters of sentences spell the key -- acrostic, 3 feathers), Murder (section headings spell the key, 5 feathers). Finding a Golden Key earns Crow Feathers; feathers convert to MARKS (governance currency). Non-zero-sum: when one person finds a key, it does NOT disappear. Keys INCREASE in value as more people find them (Glowing tier mechanics). Re-stamping: revisit after 7/30/90 days for 25%/10%/5% repeat feathers. Five document circles: Press Articles, Influencer Letters, Academic Papers, Critique Responses, Cue Cards. Milestone bonuses: 100 MARKS (first key), 500 MARKS + Badge (10 keys), Medallion + 5,000 MARKS (Grand Slam -- all 45 keys). Comprehension questions also available (3-7 feathers; worth MORE than pattern keys).
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: 12 hits at phase_0. BP020 candlepower canon adds measurable engagement throughput framing -- Code Breakers generates substrate-verified attention signals at scale.
composing_primitives: Golden Key keyword system (hidden in plain text)
Crow Feathers (intermediate unit before MARKS conversion)
MARKS governance currency (earned through effort, not purchased)
Non-zero-sum reward design (no scarcity of keys)
Midas Touch tokens (Glowing key -> Credit-to-Joule conversion ability)
founder_voice_quotes: "Your attention has value. We are paying for it."
"It is a virtuous cycle, not an extraction machine."
"Nobody's gain is anybody's loss."

ratification_chronology: Canonical article: ARTICLE_WHY_CODE_BREAKERS_MATTER_HUMANIZED.md. Pudding #79 The Golden Key (Bishop B062). BISHOP_GOLDEN_KEY_QUIZ_SEED.sql deployed. Migration: 20260331000004_k200_elbow_grease_cue_cards_golden_keys.sql.
definition: Glowing Golden Keys is the community-density amplification layer on top of the base Code Breakers / Golden Key system. Key visual states: Dim (0-9 finders, base value), Glowing (10-49 finders, +10%), Bright (50-99 finders, +25%), Radiant (100-499 finders, +50%), Legendary (500+ finders, +100%). Popular keys glow brighter in the interface, creating a community signal of which documents matter most. Glowing keys unlock Midas Touch tokens -- stored abilities to convert 1 Credit into 1 Joule. Midas token scale: Glowing (50+ finders) = 1 token, Bright (100+) = 2, Radiant (500+) = 3, Legendary (1,000+) = 5. The 24-hour post-revelation of the clue drives re-engagement. Rewards are deliberately modest -- the mechanic creates value for participants instead of extracting it.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: 17 hits confirmed. Trail Head eblet (BP015) positions Trail Heads / Cue Cards / Deck Cards as primary discovery path -- Glowing Key glow-tier becomes the substrate-native community signal per Trail Head architecture.
composing_primitives: Midas Touch token system (Credit-to-Joule conversion right)
Joule as ownership container (same material as Credits but holds things)
Battery Dispatch integration (11 of 15 posts contain keys)
Non-zero-sum economics (finding by one does not prevent finding by others)
founder_voice_quotes: "The Glow comes from the community. The Touch is yours to use."
"Engagement mechanics can create value for participants without extracting it."

ratification_chronology: Base deck_cards table: migration 20260223000007. Deck Card System expansion (B089/B090): migration 20260408000001. Portal Archway: K422 (B102). Triple Double + Auto Shop: K421 (B102). Multilingual Deck Cards: K373/B091. Trail Head unification: BP015 eblet trail_head_cue_card_deck_golden_key_bp015.eblet.md.
definition: Deck Cards are the visual, interactive navigation layer of the Liana Banyan platform -- the primary way members navigate portals, discover content, and unlock access. Each Deck Card has: card_code (unique), name, front_title, back_title, back_instructions, description, front_icon, card_type (access/quest/achievement/tool), rarity (common/uncommon/rare/epic/legendary), deep_link_url, and credit_cost. The system includes: (1) member_deck table tracking which cards a member has unlocked; (2) deck_recipes for combining cards; (3) golden_keys table for member key inventory. Level system (integer, default 1) gates progressive access. Portal-specific deck cards act as keys to portal entry (Portal Archway system -- K422). The Triple Double Ladder Deck Card shows rung progression: Rung 0 \/yr -> Rung 1 \/yr -> Rung 2 \/yr -> Rung 3 \/yr. The Medallion is a special deck card variant (K144). Mirror Mirror multilingual system supports 110 languages (K373). Lock threshold: a card's lock/unlock status is determined by the member_deck join -- if no row exists for (member_id, card_id), the card is locked. Trail Head architecture (BP015) canonizes Deck Cards = Cue Cards = Trail Heads as a single primitive.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: 29 hits confirmed. Trail Head eblet (BP015) post-dates Reckoning -- adds the unified Trail Head = Deck Card = Cue Card primitive as new composing primitive. BP020 candlepower canon confirms 8cP tier fires Deck Card delivery at parallel-scale throughput.

Medallion as special Deck Card variant (K144)
Mirror Mirror multilingual system (110 languages, K373)
Auto Shop Deck Card (currency vocabulary trainer -- K421)
Trail Head = Deck Card = Cue Card (single primitive, BP015)

ratification_chronology: Three Currencies canon: project_mark_backing_oneway.md (extended BP017 with FORK doctrine). Layer 7 Forever-Stamp Joules formalized: PROMPT_KNIGHT_KNM_FOREVER_STAMP_JOULES_LAYER_7_CURRENCY_BP018.md. MTL risk matrix: PAWN_B62_VOUCHER_LEGAL_CHARACTERIZATION_FINDINGS_B099.md (Part 7). Candlepower (cP) disambiguated from Joules: candlepower_cp_lb_throughput_unit_canon_bp020.eblet.md.
definition: Three-gear currency system. CREDITS: Exchange rate \ = 1 Credit. One-Way Valve -- Credits NEVER cash out to fiat. Used for marketplace transactions. Per-person cap \,000. Closed-loop utility token; MTL risk neutralized. MARKS: Earned through effort and contribution -- NOT purchased. Effort-differential currency. One-way ratchet: value only goes up. All Marks are backed. Marks carry governance weight in the Round Table. Legal character: internal unit of account tied to member patronage history -- capital credit certificates. Voucher denominated in Marks = patronage allocation notice (Subchapter T). JOULES: Surplus / forever stamp currency. Represents stored value from excess production. IMMUTABLE face value once minted. Minted from Marks-surplus per backing rule (Pod-N Gold Tablet). Transferable. Joules = ownership containers (same cloth as Credits, but a bag can hold things). BACKED MARKS: Asset-backed cooperative equity units. HexIsle uses the same three currencies. Why three currencies do not create arbitrage: each serves a distinct function; Marks backing ratchet prevents devaluation; Credits one-way valve prevents extraction. NOTE: candlepower (cP) is NOT a currency -- it is an AI throughput measure, disambiguated in BP020.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: 22 hits. BP020 candlepower canon explicitly separates Joules (Layer 7 forever-stamp currency) from cP (AI throughput measure). Three-gear system integrity confirmed. Augur-Pricing false-positive on 8CP disambiguated here: 8CP = 8 candlepower = throughput unit, NOT membership pricing.
composing_primitives: Marks (effort-differential, all-backed, one-way ratchet, governance weight)
Joules (forever-stamp, immutable face value, minted from Marks-surplus)
WIR Bank precedent (Switzerland 1934, 92+ years cooperative multi-currency)
Subchapter T (Marks-denominated Vouchers as patronage allocation, not loans)
Pod-N Gold Tablet (Mark backing rule canonical pointer)
Midas Touch (Glowing key -> Credit-to-Joule conversion token)
founder_voice_quotes: "Credits are just spending money. Think of it like cloth vs. a bag made of the same cloth: same material, but the bag can hold things."
"Help each other help ourselves"

ratification_chronology: Pudding #93 The HexIsle (Bishop B063, April 2, 2026). TERENO_HEXISLE_COMPLETE_DOCUMENTATION.md (compiled 2025-11-26). hexIsleWorldData.ts (canonical island positions). AA_FORMAL_2029 and AA_FORMAL_1940 (specific innovations). Knight sessions: K335 (de-personification), K348 (Northern Province), K349 (Snow Gate Quest Chain).
definition: HexIsle is the flagship demonstration of the Tereno Platform -- a tabletop RPG that doubles as a Real-World Business Simulator. Core principle: HexIsle + Liana Banyan = Real-World Business Simulator with minimal cost outlay (\ membership vs. \-\ traditional). Geography: hexagonal grid where every hex has exactly 6 neighbors, all equidistant -- geometry enforces fairness structurally. South-to-north archipelago journey across 7 islands (Creative Director James Ausbin): Island 1 (Harvest) -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 (Seek, caldera ring of 5 islets) -> 6 (Magic, lost city rising from center of caldera) -> 7. Ghost World mechanic: practice/de-risk mode for real-world learning before commitment. Ghost Items: purchasable virtual items connecting to real products; 89,000+ model variants. Skills: CANNOT be purchased -- earned only through gameplay and real-world assignments. Economic model: same three currencies as main platform (Credits/Marks/Joules). HexIsle has a Spanish-language sibling: Hexislo.com. Kickstarter integration. Member-operated island storefronts. FocusShell interface: full-screen immersive layout. The hex grid geometry is not decorative -- equal adjacency enforces no inherently advantaged position by shape.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: HexIsle highest hit count in shard at 61. Density strongest here. Hexagon geometry + Ghost World = two structurally distinct Crown Jewel-class innovations. BP020 candlepower canon links to Yvaine star-shine (B074) + real-world business simulator metaphor bridge confirmed dense.
composing_primitives: Tereno Platform (F.M.C. system for game creation with real-world contract connections)
Hexagonal geometry (equal adjacency, fairness by structure)
Ghost Items vs. Skills distinction (purchasable vs. earned)
founder_voice_quotes: "A game world where the economics are real and the hexagons are the point."
"Economic systems can be designed so that participation generates value for the participant, not just for the system operator."

ratification_chronology: Pawn B62 Voucher Legal Characterization delivered April 11, 2026 (B099 ingest). 43 Parts + 12 Appendices. Open Water innovation #2240 (AA_FORMAL_2240_OPEN_WATER_B097.md) is the parent innovation. Glass Door innovation #2262 adds public visibility layer.
definition: The Voucher system (B62, Open Water architecture) is a seven-tier cooperative member advance framework. Seven tiers: Tier 0 Dinghy \-\ de minimis peer advance; Tier 1 Rowboat \-\ time-price differential; Tier 2 Canoe \-\,000 commercial loan; Tier 3 Skiff \,000-\,000 commercial loan; Tier 4 Sailboat \,000-\,000 regulated commercial loan; Tier 5 Ship \,000-\,000 private placement/regulated; Tier 6 Yacht \,000+ securities (Reg D 506(c) required). Four-layer structural defense: (1) Commercial Purpose Affidavit; (2) Time-Price Differential (4-installment max Tiers 0-2); (3) Patronage Allocation Framing (Subchapter T WNA, Tiers 2-5); (4) Securities Safe Harbor (Reg D, Tiers 5-6). SAA (governance weight) is the return -- NOT monetary profit; defeats Howey prong 3. Strongest single action: Subchapter T election (simultaneously defeats lending law, securities, creates tax advantage). Compliance cost: \-\ build + \-\/year. California 5-loan de minimis cap is a hard database trigger.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: Voucher Tiers sparsest in shard at 8 hits. Density elevated by Reckoning. BP020 adds K525 Conductor's Baton LAUNCH as downstream pipeline that activates Voucher Tier flows in cooperative member advance.
composing_primitives: Commercial Purpose Affidavit (TILA defeat at any dollar amount)
Reg D Rule 504 (Tier 5 securities safe harbor)
Reg D Rule 506(c) (Tier 6, verified accredited investors only)
California CFL 5-loan de minimis cap (database trigger)
founder_voice_quotes: "The Subchapter T election is the single highest-ROI structural action."

ratification_chronology: COUNSEL_TASK_PEDESTAL_STAKE_CONSUMER_FLOW_B111.md (B111, April 20, 2026, Founder-ratified). K427 stub staged. Open Water Howey-clean at B098. Concurrent with K423/K424/K425/K426. K431-K433 Workstream 1 phases built portal infrastructure.
definition: Pedestal Stake is an equity-adjacent instrument representing a claim on the 10% Individual Pedestals slice of the IP Load Balancing waterfall. Issuing entity: Upekrithen LLC (Wyoming, 100% owned by Founder), NOT Liana Banyan Corporation. This separation is structurally critical: Pedestal Stakes are securities; LB cooperative membership (\/year) is not. Two-track economy: Marks (cooperative governance) and Pedestal Stakes (investment) are completely separate instruments. Holding a Pedestal Stake conveys NO LB cooperative voting rights. Four SEC exemption paths: Reg D 506(b), Reg D 506(c), Reg CF (up to \/12mo, general public), Reg A+ (up to \). Bishop recommendation: Reg CF for consumer flow + Reg D 506(b) for accredited insiders. Howey-clean per Path A / Open Water / SAA framework. Consumer flow infrastructure target Q3 2026: signup -> KYC -> payment -> stake-issuance -> investor statement dashboard. Public communication guardrail: do NOT offer Pedestal Stakes until Form filed. \-\.3B valuation projection is internal-tracking only. Herjavec RPA: 50% of Founder-Creator 20% slice = 10% of total, 20-year window.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: Pedestal Stake at 9 hits -- sparse but structurally critical. IP waterfall separation (Upekrithen LLC vs LB Corp) is a Crown Jewel isolation mechanism. BP020 Bushel 2 confirms Pedestal Stake portal (K427-K433) infrastructure in LANDED state.

ratification_chronology: Canonical ratification: ARTICLE_WHY_CODE_BREAKERS_MATTER_HUMANIZED.md (two engagement modes: Pattern Keys and Comprehension Questions). Pudding #79 The Golden Key (Bishop B062). Implementation: TreasureMapKnowledgeQuiz.tsx and treasureMapEngine.ts. Trail Head extension: trail_head_cue_card_deck_golden_key_bp015.eblet.md (post-Reckoning).
definition: Puzzle and code design patterns in the Liana Banyan ecosystem serve engagement without extraction. Three-tier difficulty architecture: Fledgling (word appears naturally -- hidden in plain sight), Flight (first letters of sentences spell the key -- acrostic pattern), Murder (section headings spell the key -- structural pattern). Comprehension questions supplement pattern keys: Basic (3 feathers -- e.g., 'What percentage goes to creators?'), Applied (5 feathers -- e.g., 'Why does Cost+20% eliminate the race to the bottom?'), Synthesis (7 feathers -- e.g., 'How does the Boaz Principle differ from charity?'). Battery Dispatch 15-post sequence with 11 embedded Golden Keys. 24-hour hint revelation drives return engagement. Grand Slam achievement (all 45 keys across five circles = Medallion + 5,000 MARKS). Non-zero-sum: keys do not disappear after first find. Five Circles: Press Articles, Influencer Letters, Academic Papers, Critique Responses, Cue Cards. Design philosophy: key submission proves careful reading vs. vanity metrics (page views prove nothing). Community scouts: finders share, creating conversations, creating community. Trail Head architecture (BP015) integrates puzzle patterns -- every Trail Head can embed a Golden Key, making the entire Cephas Proper knowledge graph puzzle-embedded at scale.
bp020_scaling_notes: 8CP parallel validation: puzzle patterns at 10 hits -- at phase_0 threshold. BP015 Trail Head architecture extends puzzle patterns to full Cephas Proper knowledge graph. Every Trail Head can embed a Golden Key, scaling the puzzle corpus from 45 keys to potentially 115+ (canonical Trail Head count).
composing_primitives: Crow Feathers (intermediate conversion unit, 1-7 per key)
Grand Slam achievement (45 keys = Medallion + 5,000 MARKS)
Community scouts (finders share -> conversations -> community -> value)
Trail Head per-topic key embedding (BP015 extension)
founder_voice_quotes: "A thousand page views tells us nothing. A hundred key submissions tells us people are reading carefully enough to notice patterns."
"Anyone can find a hidden word with enough patience. But answering why does wealth re-accumulation threaten cooperative models requires actual engagement with the ideas."
